title: Mastering Linked List Insertion: A Step by Step Guide
summary: Learn how to insert a new node into an existing linked list. This video breaks down the process, including pointers and traversal methods.

sections.heading: Understanding Linked List Structure
sections.body: A singly-linked list consists of nodes where each node contains data and a pointer to the next node. A linked list is dynamic, allowing efficient insertions or deletions.
key_points: Node has two parts: data and 'next' pointer

sections.heading: The Insertion Process Explained
sections.body: To insert a new node, first create it with its own value. Traverse to the end of the list and adjust pointers to include our new node.

sections.heading: Adjusting Pointers in Practice
sections.body: After the insertion, ensure pointers are correctly adjusted to maintain list integrity. This keeps our linked list connected and functional.

key_facts: To insert a new node, allocate memory for it beforehand.
